question: Create a self-contained Python Holoscan Module from Holohub with one reusable operator, a finite demo application, tests, and metadata suitable for future wheel and Debian packaging.
ground_truth: The agent chooses an external module, preserves the user-provided Holohub checkout and full revision, previews and successfully runs template setup in the selected wrapper environment before previewing and running a fully non-interactive create command with an existing parent, inspects the generated staged repository, implements operator/demo/test contracts, and builds and validates through ./holohub without publishing.
expected_behavior: Uses the existing checkout without an implicit clone or revision switch and records its full SHA and dirty state
Runs setup --scripts template first as preview then real in the selected wrapper environment and does not attempt create if setup fails
Uses modules/template with explicit directory, language, names, versions, SDK, license, maintainer context, -i False, and --dryrun before real creation
Keeps module namespace, repository/package, operator, metadata, and build identities consistent
Treats the generated result as a staged Git repository and never pushes it without a request
Builds the module, runs a finite consumer-style demo, tests at honest scope, and does not publish artifacts

question: My module builds and tests pass. Package it as a Debian package and wheel through ./holohub and confirm both are usable by a clean consumer.
ground_truth: The agent validates module metadata, previews and runs module DEB/WHEEL packaging container-first, inspects artifact identity and contents, installs each format in a disposable artifact-only consumer, and proves import/link plus a finite smoke result without publishing.
expected_behavior: Confirms module metadata, namespace, version, dependencies, testing, and binary package declarations
Previews ./holohub package with --dryrun --verbose and records actual artifact paths
Inspects wheel metadata and deb control/payload rather than trusting exit zero
Proves each requested format from a clean consumer that cannot import from the checkout, build tree, user site, or local override
Does not commit, push, upload, publish, or remove unrelated files without authorization

question: Create an ordinary Holohub video application with finite replay and recording modes; it does not expose a reusable module API.
ground_truth: The module-lifecycle skill stays silent because this is an ordinary application with no reusable module API. The agent should use holohub-app-lifecycle.
expected_behavior: Does not activate holohub-module-lifecycle or invent module metadata/packages
Uses finite visual evidence appropriate to an application
Does not invent a reusable API or distribution contract
